mechanism_narrative: A declining or low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and lowers the relative attractiveness of cash/bonds, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following month. This is an always-on level/trend effect independent of specific triggers.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level/trend and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or high-FEDFUNDS periods do not show significantly lower returns than low-FEDFUNDS periods (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate consistent with early recession dynamics (Sahm-rule-like threshold) signals deteriorating labor demand that leads earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over the following quarter. The signal is inactive unless the threshold is crossed.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5pp above its 12-month trailing minimum
regime_hint: labor market turning point / early recession
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when the UNRATE threshold triggers, or Rank IC conditional on trigger < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of a high nominal policy rate combined with decelerating inflation implies a rising real policy rate, tightening effective financial conditions beyond what either series indicates alone, and depresses equity valuations over the next month. Neither FEDFUNDS nor CPIAUCSL alone captures this jointly-determined real-rate tightening effect.
candidate_trigger: estimated real rate (FEDFUNDS minus 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation) > 2%
failure_condition: Forward 20d returns following high real-rate joint signal are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p>0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime (elevated FEDFUNDS), a sustained decline in housing starts signals broader interest-rate-sensitive demand destruction that precedes weaker consumer and corporate spending, leading to negative equity returns over the following quarter. Outside tightening regimes, housing starts declines carry weaker predictive power because they may reflect supply-side rebalancing rather than demand contraction.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 10% over trailing 6 months while FEDFUNDS > 4%
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns after triggered HOUST decline in tightening regime are not significantly negative (p>0.05), or the effect reverses sign in more than one out of three tightening episodes tested.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding real economic output and supports near-term corporate earnings expectations, producing a modest positive drift in equity prices over the following trading week. This is a continuous always-on trend relationship.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO trend and forward 5-day returns stays below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth below a threshold rate signals cooling labor demand ahead of broader economic slowdown, which markets price in over the subsequent month via reduced growth expectations, producing negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following triggered PAYEMS deceleration are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer spending momentum, which supports near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces a short-horizon positive drift in equity prices over the following trading week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month growth and forward 5-day returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth and industrial production jointly decelerate simultaneously, the combined signal indicates a broad-based, cross-validated slowdown in aggregate output rather than a single noisy series move, leading to a sustained negative equity drift over the following quarter. Neither series alone reliably signals this because GDPC1 is high-revision-risk and low-frequency while INDPRO is noisier at short horizons.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 1% and INDPRO YoY growth < 0%
regime_hint: broad-based growth slowdown / late-cycle contraction risk
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following joint GDPC1/INDPRO deceleration trigger are not significantly negative (p>0.05) across at least two distinct historical episodes, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
